# M4B Audiobook Tagging Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Fix M4B files generated by Shisho so audiobook players (Bound, Overcast, Plex) display the book title correctly, store series info in player-friendly atoms, always tag as audiobook, and duplicate the description into `©cmt`.

**Architecture:**
- Writer (`pkg/mp4/writer.go`) changes the atom layout: `©alb` = book title (always), `©grp` = "Series Name #N" when series exists, `com.apple.iTunes:SERIES` / `SERIES-PART` freeform atoms when series exists, `stik` = 2 always (audiobook).
- Generator (`pkg/filegen/m4b.go`) forces `MediaType = 2` and mirrors `book.Description` into `Metadata.Comment` so the writer emits `©cmt`.
- Reader (`pkg/mp4/reader.go` + `pkg/mp4/metadata.go`) adds `©grp` extraction and parses series from `com.apple.iTunes:SERIES` / `SERIES-PART` first, then `©grp`. `©alb` is no longer a series source.

**Tech Stack:** Go, `github.com/abema/go-mp4`, testify assertions, ffmpeg (via `internal/testgen`) for integration fixtures.

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-21-m4b-audiobook-tagging-design.md`

---

## File Structure

**Modified:**
- `pkg/mp4/reader.go` — add `grouping` field on `rawMetadata`; process `AtomGrouping` into it.
- `pkg/mp4/metadata.go` — change series parsing priority; remove album-as-series-source.
- `pkg/mp4/writer.go` — `buildIlst` writes `©alb` = title, `©grp` + SERIES/SERIES-PART freeforms when series set; drop `formatAlbumFromSeries` callsite for album; skip SERIES freeform keys in the generic freeform loop.
- `pkg/filegen/m4b.go` — force `MediaType = 2`; set `meta.Comment` from `book.Description`; set `meta.Album` = title.
- `pkg/mp4/mp4_test.go` — update `TestParse_SeriesExtraction` (grouping, not album); update `TestWrite_SeriesFormatting` to assert grouping/freeform; add new tests for SERIES freeform roundtrip, stik=2 forcing, album-equals-title.
- `pkg/filegen/m4b_test.go` — update the two series subtests to assert grouping/freeform/title-as-album; add tests for `stik=2` and `©cmt` from description.
- `pkg/mp4/CLAUDE.md` — update atom tables.

**No new files.**

---

## Task 1: Reader reads `©grp` Grouping atom

**Files:**
- Modify: `pkg/mp4/reader.go`
- Test: `pkg/mp4/mp4_test.go`

Current state: `isMetadataAtom` lists `AtomGrouping` (reader.go:386) but `processMetadataAtom` never populates it. We need a `grouping` field on `rawMetadata` and a case in the switch.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing reader test**

Add to `pkg/mp4/mp4_test.go` (append near the other `TestParse_*` tests, around line 103):

```go
// TestParse_GroupingExtraction verifies the ©grp atom is extracted.
// We exercise it by round-tripping through the writer: write a Metadata
// struct with Series + SeriesNumber, then re-parse and expect the series
// to come through via grouping.
func TestParse_GroupingExtraction(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()
	testgen.SkipIfNoFFmpeg(t)
	dir := testgen.TempDir(t, "mp4-grouping-*")

	path := testgen.GenerateM4B(t, dir, "test.m4b", testgen.M4BOptions{
		Title:    "Book Title",
		Duration: 1.0,
	})

	// Write a file with series set; the new writer emits ©grp.
	meta, err := mp4.ParseFull(path)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	num := 7.0
	meta.Series = "Dungeon Crawler Carl"
	meta.SeriesNumber = &num

	err = mp4.Write(path, meta, mp4.WriteOptions{})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	reparsed, err := mp4.ParseFull(path)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "Dungeon Crawler Carl", reparsed.Series)
	require.NotNil(t, reparsed.SeriesNumber)
	assert.InDelta(t, 7.0, *reparsed.SeriesNumber, 0.001)
	// Album must be the title, not the series-formatted string.
	assert.Equal(t, "Book Title", reparsed.Album)
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the test to verify it fails**

Run: `go test ./pkg/mp4/ -run TestParse_GroupingExtraction -v`

Expected: FAIL. `reparsed.Album` will be empty (or equal to whatever the old album flow wrote) and `reparsed.Series` will be empty — the new grouping emission and parsing do not exist yet. This test is the integration target for Tasks 1–4; you'll see it go green after Task 4.

- [ ] **Step 3: Add `grouping` field to `rawMetadata`**

In `pkg/mp4/reader.go`, extend the struct at lines 15–40:

```go
type rawMetadata struct {
	title        string
	artist       string
	album        string
	grouping     string // from ©grp (used as a series source)
	narrator     string // from ©nrt (dedicated narrator atom)
	composer     string // from ©cmp
	writer       string // from ©wrt (ffmpeg uses this for composer)
	genre        string
	description  string
	comment      string // from ©cmt
	year         string // from ©day
	copyright    string // from ©cpy
	encoder      string // from ©too
	publisher    string // from ©pub
	releaseDate  string // from rldt (Audible release date)
	coverData    []byte
	coverMime    string
	mediaType    int64
	timescale    uint32            // from mvhd - units per second
	duration     uint64            // from mvhd - in timescale units
	avgBitrate   uint32            // from esds - average bitrate in bps
	codec        string            // from esds - audio codec name with profile (e.g., "AAC-LC", "xHE-AAC")
	freeform     map[string]string // freeform (----) atoms like com.apple.iTunes:ASIN
	chapters     []Chapter         // chapter list
	unknownAtoms []RawAtom         // unrecognized atoms to preserve
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Process the AtomGrouping atom**

In `pkg/mp4/reader.go`, add a case to `processMetadataAtom` (insert alongside the other cases, near the existing `AtomAlbum` case around line 574):

```go
	case atomTypeEquals(boxType, AtomGrouping):
		meta.grouping = parseTextData(child.data)
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add pkg/mp4/reader.go pkg/mp4/mp4_test.go
git commit -m "[Backend] Parse ©grp grouping atom in M4B reader"
```

The test added in Step 1 is expected to still fail after this commit — it exercises writer behavior that will land in Task 3.

## Task 2: Series parsing priority (freeform → grouping)

**Files:**
- Modify: `pkg/mp4/metadata.go`
- Test: `pkg/mp4/metadata_test.go`

Replace the current album-parses-series behavior with the new priority:
1. `com.apple.iTunes:SERIES` + `com.apple.iTunes:SERIES-PART` freeform atoms.
2. `©grp` Grouping.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing unit tests**

Add to `pkg/mp4/metadata_test.go`:

```go
// TestConvertRawMetadata_SeriesFromFreeform tests that series is parsed from
// the Audible-style freeform SERIES / SERIES-PART atoms.
func TestConvertRawMetadata_SeriesFromFreeform(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()

	t.Run("freeform SERIES and SERIES-PART", func(t *testing.T) {
		raw := &rawMetadata{
			freeform: map[string]string{
				"com.apple.iTunes:SERIES":      "Expanse",
				"com.apple.iTunes:SERIES-PART": "3",
			},
		}
		meta := convertRawMetadata(raw)
		assert.Equal(t, "Expanse", meta.Series)
		require.NotNil(t, meta.SeriesNumber)
		assert.InDelta(t, 3.0, *meta.SeriesNumber, 0.001)
	})

	t.Run("freeform SERIES with decimal SERIES-PART", func(t *testing.T) {
		raw := &rawMetadata{
			freeform: map[string]string{
				"com.apple.iTunes:SERIES":      "Expanse",
				"com.apple.iTunes:SERIES-PART": "3.5",
			},
		}
		meta := convertRawMetadata(raw)
		assert.Equal(t, "Expanse", meta.Series)
		require.NotNil(t, meta.SeriesNumber)
		assert.InDelta(t, 3.5, *meta.SeriesNumber, 0.001)
	})

	t.Run("freeform SERIES without SERIES-PART", func(t *testing.T) {
		raw := &rawMetadata{
			freeform: map[string]string{
				"com.apple.iTunes:SERIES": "Expanse",
			},
		}
		meta := convertRawMetadata(raw)
		assert.Equal(t, "Expanse", meta.Series)
		assert.Nil(t, meta.SeriesNumber)
	})

	t.Run("freeform beats grouping", func(t *testing.T) {
		raw := &rawMetadata{
			grouping: "Fallback Series #9",
			freeform: map[string]string{
				"com.apple.iTunes:SERIES":      "Preferred Series",
				"com.apple.iTunes:SERIES-PART": "1",
			},
		}
		meta := convertRawMetadata(raw)
		assert.Equal(t, "Preferred Series", meta.Series)
		require.NotNil(t, meta.SeriesNumber)
		assert.InDelta(t, 1.0, *meta.SeriesNumber, 0.001)
	})
}

// TestConvertRawMetadata_SeriesFromGrouping tests that series falls back to
// parsing from the ©grp grouping atom when no freeform SERIES exists.
func TestConvertRawMetadata_SeriesFromGrouping(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()

	t.Run("grouping with hash format", func(t *testing.T) {
		raw := &rawMetadata{grouping: "Dungeon Crawler Carl #7"}
		meta := convertRawMetadata(raw)
		assert.Equal(t, "Dungeon Crawler Carl", meta.Series)
		require.NotNil(t, meta.SeriesNumber)
		assert.InDelta(t, 7.0, *meta.SeriesNumber, 0.001)
	})

	t.Run("grouping with book format", func(t *testing.T) {
		raw := &rawMetadata{grouping: "Mistborn, Book 3"}
		meta := convertRawMetadata(raw)
		assert.Equal(t, "Mistborn", meta.Series)
		require.NotNil(t, meta.SeriesNumber)
		assert.InDelta(t, 3.0, *meta.SeriesNumber, 0.001)
	})
}

// TestConvertRawMetadata_AlbumIsNotSeriesSource verifies that series is NOT
// extracted from the album atom. Existing files that stored series in album
// (from older Shisho versions) will lose their series on re-scan; series data
// still lives in the Shisho database.
func TestConvertRawMetadata_AlbumIsNotSeriesSource(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()
	raw := &rawMetadata{album: "Some Series #2"}
	meta := convertRawMetadata(raw)
	assert.Equal(t, "Some Series #2", meta.Album) // album still readable
	assert.Empty(t, meta.Series)
	assert.Nil(t, meta.SeriesNumber)
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the tests to verify they fail**

Run: `go test ./pkg/mp4/ -run 'TestConvertRawMetadata_SeriesFromFreeform|TestConvertRawMetadata_SeriesFromGrouping|TestConvertRawMetadata_AlbumIsNotSeriesSource' -v`

Expected: all three FAIL. The existing code only parses series from `raw.album` (metadata.go:102–107).

- [ ] **Step 3: Replace the series parsing block**

In `pkg/mp4/metadata.go`, replace lines 101–107 (the current "Parse series information from album field" block) with:

```go
	// Parse series information.
	// Priority:
	//   1. Audible-style freeform atoms: com.apple.iTunes:SERIES + SERIES-PART
	//   2. ©grp Grouping atom (format: "Series Name #N", "Series Name, Book N", etc.)
	// ©alb Album is intentionally NOT a series source — the writer now uses
	// album for the book title, so parsing series from it would be wrong.
	if name, ok := raw.freeform["com.apple.iTunes:SERIES"]; ok && name != "" {
		meta.Series = strings.TrimSpace(name)
		if part, ok := raw.freeform["com.apple.iTunes:SERIES-PART"]; ok && part != "" {
			if num, err := strconv.ParseFloat(strings.TrimSpace(part), 64); err == nil {
				meta.SeriesNumber = &num
			}
		}
	} else if raw.grouping != "" {
		if parsed := parseSeriesFromGrouping(raw.grouping); parsed.series != "" {
			meta.Series = parsed.series
			meta.SeriesNumber = parsed.number
		}
	}
```

The `strings` and `strconv` imports are already present in `metadata.go`; no import changes needed.

- [ ] **Step 4: Run the tests to verify they pass**

Run: `go test ./pkg/mp4/ -run 'TestConvertRawMetadata_SeriesFromFreeform|TestConvertRawMetadata_SeriesFromGrouping|TestConvertRawMetadata_AlbumIsNotSeriesSource' -v`

Expected: PASS.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add pkg/mp4/metadata.go pkg/mp4/metadata_test.go
git commit -m "[Backend] Parse M4B series from freeform/grouping, not album"
```

## Task 3: Writer emits grouping + SERIES freeforms, album = title, always stik=2

**Files:**
- Modify: `pkg/mp4/writer.go`
- Test: `pkg/mp4/mp4_test.go`

- [ ] **Step 1: Update the writer's series tests to the new atom layout**

In `pkg/mp4/mp4_test.go`, replace the existing `TestWrite_SeriesFormatting` (currently at ~line 213, asserts `modified.Album == "Test Series #1"`) with:

```go
// TestWrite_SeriesAtoms tests that series is written to ©grp and to the
// Audible-style SERIES / SERIES-PART freeform atoms, and that ©alb is the
// book title (not the series).
func TestWrite_SeriesAtoms(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()
	testgen.SkipIfNoFFmpeg(t)

	tests := []struct {
		name            string
		series          string
		seriesNumber    *float64
		expectedGroup   string
		expectedPart    string
	}{
		{"integer number", "Test Series", pointerutil.Float64(1), "Test Series #1", "1"},
		{"decimal number", "Test Series", pointerutil.Float64(1.5), "Test Series #1.5", "1.5"},
		{"no number", "Test Series", nil, "Test Series", ""},
	}

	for _, tc := range tests {
		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
			dir := testgen.TempDir(t, "mp4-series-atoms-*")

			path := testgen.GenerateM4B(t, dir, "test.m4b", testgen.M4BOptions{
				Title:    "Book Title",
				Duration: 1.0,
			})

			meta, err := mp4.ParseFull(path)
			require.NoError(t, err)
			meta.Series = tc.series
			meta.SeriesNumber = tc.seriesNumber

			err = mp4.Write(path, meta, mp4.WriteOptions{})
			require.NoError(t, err)

			modified, err := mp4.ParseFull(path)
			require.NoError(t, err)

			// Album must be the book title, never the series.
			assert.Equal(t, "Book Title", modified.Album)

			// Series round-trips via freeform SERIES + SERIES-PART.
			assert.Equal(t, tc.series, modified.Series)
			if tc.seriesNumber != nil {
				require.NotNil(t, modified.SeriesNumber)
				assert.InDelta(t, *tc.seriesNumber, *modified.SeriesNumber, 0.001)
			} else {
				assert.Nil(t, modified.SeriesNumber)
			}

			// Raw freeform map should contain the Audible-style atoms.
			require.NotNil(t, modified.Freeform)
			assert.Equal(t, tc.series, modified.Freeform["com.apple.iTunes:SERIES"])
			if tc.expectedPart == "" {
				_, hasPart := modified.Freeform["com.apple.iTunes:SERIES-PART"]
				assert.False(t, hasPart, "SERIES-PART should be absent when no number")
			} else {
				assert.Equal(t, tc.expectedPart, modified.Freeform["com.apple.iTunes:SERIES-PART"])
			}
		})
	}
}

// TestWrite_AlbumIsTitleWhenNoSeries verifies that ©alb equals the title
// even when the book has no series.
func TestWrite_AlbumIsTitleWhenNoSeries(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()
	testgen.SkipIfNoFFmpeg(t)
	dir := testgen.TempDir(t, "mp4-album-title-*")

	path := testgen.GenerateM4B(t, dir, "test.m4b", testgen.M4BOptions{
		Title:    "Yesteryear",
		Duration: 1.0,
	})

	meta, err := mp4.ParseFull(path)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	// No series set.
	meta.Series = ""
	meta.SeriesNumber = nil
	// Simulate what the generator does: force album to title.
	meta.Album = meta.Title

	err = mp4.Write(path, meta, mp4.WriteOptions{})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	modified, err := mp4.ParseFull(path)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "Yesteryear", modified.Album)
	assert.Empty(t, modified.Series)
	_, hasSeries := modified.Freeform["com.apple.iTunes:SERIES"]
	assert.False(t, hasSeries)
}

// TestWrite_ForcesAudiobookStik verifies that stik is emitted when set to 2,
// even if the source had no stik atom. This is the writer-level guarantee;
// the generator layer (pkg/filegen/m4b.go) is what forces MediaType=2.
func TestWrite_ForcesAudiobookStik(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()
	testgen.SkipIfNoFFmpeg(t)
	dir := testgen.TempDir(t, "mp4-stik-*")

	path := testgen.GenerateM4B(t, dir, "test.m4b", testgen.M4BOptions{
		Title:    "Stik Test",
		Duration: 1.0,
	})

	// Baseline: ffmpeg-generated file has no stik (MediaType == 0).
	baseline, err := mp4.ParseFull(path)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, 0, baseline.MediaType)

	baseline.MediaType = 2
	err = mp4.Write(path, baseline, mp4.WriteOptions{})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	modified, err := mp4.ParseFull(path)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, 2, modified.MediaType)
}
```

Remove the old `TestWrite_SeriesFormatting` (it asserts `modified.Album == "Test Series #1"`, which is the exact behavior we're changing).

- [ ] **Step 2: Update `TestParse_SeriesExtraction` to not use album**

The existing `TestParse_SeriesExtraction` in `pkg/mp4/mp4_test.go` (~line 84) uses `Album: "Dungeon Crawler Carl #7"` which relied on album-as-series-source. Replace its body with a round-trip through the writer's new series atoms:

```go
func TestParse_SeriesExtraction(t *testing.T) {
	t.Parallel()
	testgen.SkipIfNoFFmpeg(t)
	dir := testgen.TempDir(t, "mp4-series-*")

	path := testgen.GenerateM4B(t, dir, "test.m4b", testgen.M4BOptions{
		Title:    "Book Title",
		Artist:   "Author",
		Duration: 1.0,
	})

	// Round-trip through the writer so we exercise the real atom layout.
	meta, err := mp4.ParseFull(path)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	num := 7.0
	meta.Series = "Dungeon Crawler Carl"
	meta.SeriesNumber = &num
	require.NoError(t, mp4.Write(path, meta, mp4.WriteOptions{}))

	metadata, err := mp4.Parse(path)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Equal(t, "Dungeon Crawler Carl", metadata.Series)
	require.NotNil(t, metadata.SeriesNumber)
	assert.InDelta(t, 7.0, *metadata.SeriesNumber, 0.001)
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Run tests to confirm failures**

Run: `go test ./pkg/mp4/ -run 'TestWrite_SeriesAtoms|TestWrite_AlbumIsTitleWhenNoSeries|TestWrite_ForcesAudiobookStik|TestParse_SeriesExtraction|TestParse_GroupingExtraction' -v`

Expected: all FAIL — the writer still puts series into `©alb` via `formatAlbumFromSeries` and does not emit `©grp` or SERIES freeform atoms.

- [ ] **Step 4: Update `buildIlst` to write album=title, grouping, and SERIES freeforms**

In `pkg/mp4/writer.go`, replace the album block (currently lines 299–306 inside `buildIlst`) with:

```go
	// Album: always the book title. Audiobook players (Bound, Overcast, etc.)
	// commonly use Album as the canonical book-title atom; leaving it empty or
	// putting series info here makes those players show "Unknown". Series info
	// goes into ©grp and the Audible-style SERIES / SERIES-PART freeforms
	// below instead.
	if metadata.Album != "" {
		content.Write(buildItunesTextAtom(AtomAlbum, metadata.Album))
	}

	// Series info: write to ©grp (legacy/compatibility) and to the Audible-
	// style freeform atoms com.apple.iTunes:SERIES + SERIES-PART (preferred
	// modern source, used by Audible, Tone, Audiobookshelf).
	if metadata.Series != "" {
		grouping := formatSeriesGrouping(metadata.Series, metadata.SeriesNumber)
		content.Write(buildItunesTextAtom(AtomGrouping, grouping))
		content.Write(buildFreeformAtom("com.apple.iTunes", "SERIES", metadata.Series))
		if metadata.SeriesNumber != nil {
			content.Write(buildFreeformAtom("com.apple.iTunes", "SERIES-PART", formatSeriesNumber(*metadata.SeriesNumber)))
		}
	}
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Replace `formatAlbumFromSeries` with `formatSeriesGrouping` + `formatSeriesNumber`**

In `pkg/mp4/writer.go`, replace the `formatAlbumFromSeries` function (lines 500–513) with:

```go
// formatSeriesGrouping formats series info as a grouping string: "Series Name #N".
func formatSeriesGrouping(series string, number *float64) string {
	if series == "" {
		return ""
	}
	if number == nil {
		return series
	}
	return series + " #" + formatSeriesNumber(*number)
}

// formatSeriesNumber formats a series number as integer when whole, decimal otherwise.
func formatSeriesNumber(num float64) string {
	if num == float64(int(num)) {
		return strconv.Itoa(int(num))
	}
	return strconv.FormatFloat(num, 'f', -1, 64)
}
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Skip SERIES freeform keys in the generic loop**

In `pkg/mp4/writer.go`, extend the `explicitFreeformKeys` map in `buildIlst` (currently lines 350–356) to include the new keys so they aren't double-written by the catch-all freeform loop:

```go
	explicitFreeformKeys := map[string]bool{
		"com.apple.iTunes:SUBTITLE":     true,
		"com.pilabor.tone:SUBTITLE":     true,
		"com.shisho:tags":               true,
		"com.apple.iTunes:ASIN":         true,
		"com.pilabor.tone:AUDIBLE_ASIN": true,
		"com.apple.iTunes:SERIES":       true,
		"com.apple.iTunes:SERIES-PART":  true,
	}
```

Run: `go test ./pkg/mp4/ -v`

Expected: all tests in `pkg/mp4/` PASS, including the new ones from Task 1–3.

## Task 4: Generator forces stik=2, mirrors description into ©cmt, sets album = title

**Files:**
- Modify: `pkg/filegen/m4b.go`
- Test: `pkg/filegen/m4b_test.go`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing integration tests**

In `pkg/filegen/m4b_test.go`, update the two series subtests and add new subtests. First, replace the `"formats series as album"` subtest (line 133) and the `"handles decimal series number"` subtest (line 168) with:

```go
	t.Run("writes series to grouping and freeform atoms", func(t *testing.T) {
		testgen.SkipIfNoFFmpeg(t)
		dir := testgen.TempDir(t, "m4b-gen-*")

		srcPath := testgen.GenerateM4B(t, dir, "source.m4b", testgen.M4BOptions{
			Title:    "Test Book",
			Duration: 1.0,
		})

		destPath := filepath.Join(dir, "dest.m4b")

		book := &models.Book{
			Title: "Test Book",
			Authors: []*models.Author{
				{SortOrder: 0, Person: &models.Person{Name: "Author"}},
			},
			BookSeries: []*models.BookSeries{
				{SortOrder: 0, SeriesNumber: pointerutil.Float64(3), Series: &models.Series{Name: "Test Series"}},
			},
		}
		file := &models.File{FileType: models.FileTypeM4B}

		gen := &M4BGenerator{}
		err := gen.Generate(context.Background(), srcPath, destPath, book, file)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		meta, err := mp4.ParseFull(destPath)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		assert.Equal(t, "Test Series", meta.Series)
		require.NotNil(t, meta.SeriesNumber)
		assert.InDelta(t, 3.0, *meta.SeriesNumber, 0.001)
		// Album is the title, not the series.
		assert.Equal(t, "Test Book", meta.Album)
		// Audible-style freeform atoms are set.
		require.NotNil(t, meta.Freeform)
		assert.Equal(t, "Test Series", meta.Freeform["com.apple.iTunes:SERIES"])
		assert.Equal(t, "3", meta.Freeform["com.apple.iTunes:SERIES-PART"])
	})

	t.Run("handles decimal series number", func(t *testing.T) {
		testgen.SkipIfNoFFmpeg(t)
		dir := testgen.TempDir(t, "m4b-gen-*")

		srcPath := testgen.GenerateM4B(t, dir, "source.m4b", testgen.M4BOptions{
			Title:    "Test Book",
			Duration: 1.0,
		})

		destPath := filepath.Join(dir, "dest.m4b")

		book := &models.Book{
			Title: "Test Book",
			Authors: []*models.Author{
				{SortOrder: 0, Person: &models.Person{Name: "Author"}},
			},
			BookSeries: []*models.BookSeries{
				{SortOrder: 0, SeriesNumber: pointerutil.Float64(1.5), Series: &models.Series{Name: "Series"}},
			},
		}
		file := &models.File{FileType: models.FileTypeM4B}

		gen := &M4BGenerator{}
		err := gen.Generate(context.Background(), srcPath, destPath, book, file)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		meta, err := mp4.ParseFull(destPath)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.NotNil(t, meta.SeriesNumber)
		assert.InDelta(t, 1.5, *meta.SeriesNumber, 0.001)
		assert.Equal(t, "1.5", meta.Freeform["com.apple.iTunes:SERIES-PART"])
		// Album is still the title.
		assert.Equal(t, "Test Book", meta.Album)
	})

	t.Run("always tags as audiobook (stik=2)", func(t *testing.T) {
		testgen.SkipIfNoFFmpeg(t)
		dir := testgen.TempDir(t, "m4b-gen-*")

		// ffmpeg doesn't emit a stik atom by default, so the source file
		// has MediaType == 0.
		srcPath := testgen.GenerateM4B(t, dir, "source.m4b", testgen.M4BOptions{
			Title:    "Stik Test",
			Duration: 1.0,
		})
		srcMeta, err := mp4.ParseFull(srcPath)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.Equal(t, 0, srcMeta.MediaType, "precondition: source file has no stik")

		destPath := filepath.Join(dir, "dest.m4b")

		book := &models.Book{
			Title: "Stik Test",
			Authors: []*models.Author{
				{SortOrder: 0, Person: &models.Person{Name: "Author"}},
			},
		}
		file := &models.File{FileType: models.FileTypeM4B}

		gen := &M4BGenerator{}
		err = gen.Generate(context.Background(), srcPath, destPath, book, file)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		meta, err := mp4.ParseFull(destPath)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		assert.Equal(t, 2, meta.MediaType)
	})

	t.Run("writes description to both desc and ©cmt", func(t *testing.T) {
		testgen.SkipIfNoFFmpeg(t)
		dir := testgen.TempDir(t, "m4b-gen-*")

		srcPath := testgen.GenerateM4B(t, dir, "source.m4b", testgen.M4BOptions{
			Title:    "Desc Test",
			Duration: 1.0,
		})

		destPath := filepath.Join(dir, "dest.m4b")

		desc := "A twisty novel about a woman out of time."
		book := &models.Book{
			Title:       "Desc Test",
			Description: &desc,
			Authors: []*models.Author{
				{SortOrder: 0, Person: &models.Person{Name: "Author"}},
			},
		}
		file := &models.File{FileType: models.FileTypeM4B}

		gen := &M4BGenerator{}
		err := gen.Generate(context.Background(), srcPath, destPath, book, file)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		meta, err := mp4.ParseFull(destPath)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		assert.Equal(t, desc, meta.Description)
		assert.Equal(t, desc, meta.Comment)
	})

	t.Run("album equals title when no series", func(t *testing.T) {
		testgen.SkipIfNoFFmpeg(t)
		dir := testgen.TempDir(t, "m4b-gen-*")

		srcPath := testgen.GenerateM4B(t, dir, "source.m4b", testgen.M4BOptions{
			Title:    "Yesteryear",
			Duration: 1.0,
		})

		destPath := filepath.Join(dir, "dest.m4b")

		book := &models.Book{
			Title: "Yesteryear",
			Authors: []*models.Author{
				{SortOrder: 0, Person: &models.Person{Name: "Caro Claire Burke"}},
			},
		}
		file := &models.File{FileType: models.FileTypeM4B}

		gen := &M4BGenerator{}
		err := gen.Generate(context.Background(), srcPath, destPath, book, file)
		require.NoError(t, err)

		meta, err := mp4.ParseFull(destPath)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		assert.Equal(t, "Yesteryear", meta.Album)
		assert.Empty(t, meta.Series)
	})
```

The `time` import remains in use via existing subtests; no new imports required.

- [ ] **Step 2: Run tests to verify failures**

Run: `go test ./pkg/filegen/ -run TestM4BGenerator_Generate -v`

Expected: the new subtests FAIL:
- `writes series to grouping and freeform atoms`: Album will be empty (the writer no longer sets album from series, but `buildMetadata` doesn't set `meta.Album = title` yet).
- `handles decimal series number`: same — Album is empty.
- `always tags as audiobook (stik=2)`: meta.MediaType is 0 (generator preserves src.MediaType).
- `writes description to both desc and ©cmt`: meta.Comment is empty (generator only sets Description).
- `album equals title when no series`: meta.Album is empty.

- [ ] **Step 3: Update `buildMetadata` in the generator**

In `pkg/filegen/m4b.go`, change the metadata construction (currently lines 60–105). Specifically:

1. Add `meta.Album = title` so the writer emits `©alb` = book title.
2. Hardcode `MediaType: 2` instead of `MediaType: src.MediaType`.
3. When `book.Description` is set, also set `meta.Comment` to the same value (mirror of what the `desc` branch already does).

Replace the `meta := &mp4.Metadata{ ... }` literal block (lines 67–94) with:

```go
	meta := &mp4.Metadata{
		// From book/file model
		Title:    title,
		Subtitle: "",
		// Album tracks the book title. Audiobook players commonly use ©alb as
		// the canonical "book title" atom, so we always emit it. Series info
		// is written to ©grp + SERIES/SERIES-PART freeforms by the writer.
		Album: title,

		// Preserve from source (will be overwritten if we have new values)
		Description: src.Description,
		Comment:     src.Comment,
		Year:        src.Year,
		Copyright:   src.Copyright,
		Encoder:     src.Encoder,
		Duration:    src.Duration,
		Bitrate:     src.Bitrate,
		Chapters:    src.Chapters,
		Freeform:    src.Freeform,
		Publisher:   src.Publisher,
		Imprint:     src.Imprint,
		URL:         src.URL,
		ReleaseDate: src.ReleaseDate,

		// Always tag as audiobook. Some source files have no stik atom,
		// which causes audiobook players to treat them as generic music.
		MediaType: 2,

		// Preserve cover from source initially (may be replaced below)
		CoverData:     src.CoverData,
		CoverMimeType: src.CoverMimeType,

		// Preserve unknown atoms for complete tag preservation
		UnknownAtoms: src.UnknownAtoms,
	}
```

Then, update the description block (currently lines 101–104) to also write `Comment`:

```go
	// Set description from book if available. Mirror into ©cmt (Comment) too
	// so audiobook taggers/players that prefer the comment field see it.
	if book.Description != nil && *book.Description != "" {
		meta.Description = *book.Description
		meta.Comment = *book.Description
	}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run tests to verify they pass**

Run: `go test ./pkg/filegen/ -run TestM4BGenerator_Generate -v`

Expected: all subtests PASS.

Also run the full mp4 + filegen suites:

```
go test ./pkg/mp4/... ./pkg/filegen/... -v
```

Expected: all PASS.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add pkg/filegen/m4b.go pkg/filegen/m4b_test.go
git commit -m "[Backend] M4B gen: force stik=2, album=title, mirror description to ©cmt"
```

## Task 5: Update `pkg/mp4/CLAUDE.md` docs

**Files:**
- Modify: `pkg/mp4/CLAUDE.md`

The atom tables currently claim series comes from album/grouping and `©alb` is for series; update to match reality.

- [ ] **Step 1: Update the "Standard Atoms" table**

In `pkg/mp4/CLAUDE.md`, find the table starting at line ~48 (`| Atom | Code | Shisho Usage |`) and change these rows:

```
| `©alb` | Album | Book title (always) |
| `©grp` | Grouping | Series info: "Series Name #N" |
| `©cmt` | Comment | Description (written alongside desc) |
```

Leave the rest of the rows unchanged.

- [ ] **Step 2: Add SERIES freeform atoms to the freeform list**

In the "Freeform Atoms (`----`)" section (~line 69), add:

```
----:com.apple.iTunes:SERIES       # Series name (preferred source)
----:com.apple.iTunes:SERIES-PART  # Series number (preferred source)
```

Remove the now-incorrect "Audible style" qualifier from the grouping line since we now use these as our primary series source.

- [ ] **Step 3: Update the "All Metadata Fields Extracted" table**

Find the table starting at "All Metadata Fields Extracted" (~line 107) and change the series rows:

```
| Series Name | `com.apple.iTunes:SERIES` freeform → `©grp` | Freeform preferred, grouping fallback |
| Series Number | `com.apple.iTunes:SERIES-PART` freeform → `©grp` | Parsed as float |
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Update the "Metadata Written Back" table in Generation section**

Find the "Metadata Written Back" table (~line 163). Change/add these rows to match the new writer behavior:

```
| Title | `©nam` | book.Title (or file.Name) |
| Album | `©alb` | Book title (always, for audiobook player compatibility) |
| Grouping | `©grp` | "Series Name #N" when series exists |
| Series | `----:com.apple.iTunes:SERIES` | Series name when set |
| Series Part | `----:com.apple.iTunes:SERIES-PART` | Series number when set |
| Media Type | `stik` | Always `2` (audiobook) |
| Comment | `©cmt` | book.Description (mirrored from desc) |
```

Remove the existing "Album: ... Series Name #N format" row.

- [ ] **Step 5: Run the full check pipeline**

Run: `mise check:quiet`

Expected: PASS (Go tests + lints + JS checks).

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit**

```bash
git add pkg/mp4/CLAUDE.md
git commit -m "[Docs] Update pkg/mp4/CLAUDE.md for new series/album/stik behavior"
```

## Task 6: Manual verification on the reproducer file

**Files:** none

This step is a manual check to confirm the original bug is fixed.

- [ ] **Step 1: Regenerate the file**

The book "Yesteryear" already exists in the user's library (that's where the downloaded file came from). Start the dev server and redownload the M4B via the same path the user used originally:

```
mise start
```

Navigate to the book in the UI and trigger a download. Save the new file and compare atoms:

```
go run ./cmd/scripts/debug/print-mp4-atoms <path-to-new-file.m4b>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Confirm the new atoms are present**

Expected in output:
- `MediaType: 2` (not 0)
- Album is the book title ("Yesteryear")
- Comment mirrors the description

If this is a non-series book, the freeform list should NOT contain SERIES/SERIES-PART. If you pick a series book to test as well, those keys should be present with the correct values.

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify in an audiobook player**

Move the regenerated file to a device with an audiobook player (Bound, Overcast, Apple Books) and confirm the title now displays correctly instead of "Unknown".

No commit for this task — it's verification only.

## Self-Review

**Spec coverage:**
- Album = book title always → Task 3 (writer) + Task 4 (generator seeds `meta.Album`). ✓
- `©grp` = "Series Name #N" when series → Task 3. ✓
- SERIES / SERIES-PART freeform when series → Task 3. ✓
- Always `stik = 2` → Task 4 (generator). ✓
- Mirror description into `©cmt` → Task 4 (generator). ✓
- Reader series priority freeform → grouping → Task 1 (grouping read) + Task 2 (priority). ✓
- Album dropped as series source → Task 2. ✓
- Tests per the spec's Testing section → Tasks 1–4. ✓
- CLAUDE.md update per spec's Docs section → Task 5. ✓
- Manual verification of the original bug → Task 6. ✓

**Placeholder scan:** no TBDs, no "handle edge cases", every code-changing step shows the exact code.

**Type consistency:**
- `formatSeriesGrouping(series string, number *float64) string` introduced in Task 3 and only called there — consistent.
- `formatSeriesNumber(num float64) string` introduced in Task 3; used by both `formatSeriesGrouping` and by the SERIES-PART freeform write — consistent.
- `rawMetadata.grouping` added in Task 1, read in Task 2, used nowhere else — consistent.
- `Metadata.Album`, `Metadata.Series`, `Metadata.SeriesNumber`, `Metadata.Comment`, `Metadata.MediaType` are all existing fields in `pkg/mp4/metadata.go`; no new public fields.
